destinations.evidence: The port payload builder now checks Instant::now() > global_deadline before fulfilled async collection and delivery-ready collection, returning an empty vector for each phase when the deadline is exceeded. The existing sequencing-rights path already preserves tip-sorted fallback order when the same deadline prevents reordering.
destinations.snippet:
let fulfilled_async_entries = if Instant::now() > global_deadline { Vec::new() } else { ... };
let delivery_ready_async_entries = if Instant::now() > global_deadline { Vec::new() } else { ... };

equivalence_argument: Mapped in the port after the local parity fix: fulfilled async collection, delivery-ready collection, and sequencing-rights reordering are all gated by the configured global build deadline, with the existing tip-sorted fallback order retained if sequencing-rights work is skipped.

related_but_insufficient_code.evidence: Before the local parity fix, global_deadline was computed, but fulfilled async entries were collected at the pool boundary before the first deadline check around sequencing-rights reorder. The local fix adds a pre-collection fulfilled async deadline guard.
If the deadline has expired before sequencing rights, the builder preserves tip-sorted payload_candidates, satisfying the fallback-order part.

Before the local parity fix, the execution loop broke when the deadline expired, but this was after fulfilled/delivery collection and candidate preparation. The local fix adds pre-collection guards for those phases.

notes: Mapped after local parity fix: the tip-sorted sequencing fallback was already present, and fulfilled/delivery collection now also skip when the global deadline is exceeded.
rationale: The merge adds deadline guards before Phase B, Phase C, and sequencing-rights EVM simulation. If sequencing-rights work is skipped, sorting by tip still runs and `apply_sequencing_rights` returns without partially applying reordering when the deadline expires during classification. This result keeps the candidate as a distinct refined invariant because the bounded assignment packet does not contain a newer candidate that clearly supersedes it. Implementation-detail caveat: The exact placement of deadline checks is payload-builder scheduling policy; future builders could enforce equivalent deadlines with a different task scheduler.
statement: Payload building must skip fulfilled async collection, Phase 2 delivery collection, and sequencing-rights simulation when the configured global build deadline has already expired, while preserving a tip-sorted fallback order for sequencing rights.
suggested_falsification_tests: Configure a very short payload build deadline, trigger payload building with async/delivery candidates and sequencing-rights candidates, and assert those expensive phases are skipped with no partial sequencing-rights reorder while tip sorting still occurs.
summary: Build deadline handling now skips fulfilled async and delivery-ready collection phases and retains the existing sequencing-rights fallback behavior.
test_or_falsification_candidate: Payload-builder tests should cover expired-deadline Phase B/C collection skipping and sequencing-rights fallback order; helper tests cover the cap half added with this fix.
title: Async payload phases respect build deadline
